food_subgroup: Fermented napa cabbage (baechu/배추 kimchi) — salt-brined, packed with gochugaru, garlic, ginger, salted shrimp/fish sauce, fermented

ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for kimchi as a food product in available clinical terminology resources.

serving.description: Per 50g serving (standard banchan portion, approximately 3–4 tablespoons)
serving.natural_language_portion: A standard Korean banchan serving of kimchi is approximately 50g (3–4 tablespoons), providing about 20 kcal, 405mg sodium, and 1.5g dietary fibre.

nutrient_source.title: Kimchi, raw — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy FDC ID 169228, cross-checked against CJ Bibigo Original Kimchi Singapore 500g jar product label
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service, FoodData Central SR Legacy
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: USDA SR Legacy FDC 169228 (Kimchi, raw) used as primary nutrient anchor. Values cross-checked against CJ Bibigo Original Kimchi Singapore 500g commercial product label for sodium and energy confirmation. USDA values are consistent with the commercial product label within acceptable variance.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of fermented vegetable foods — analogous estimate based on University of Sydney GI Database fermented/pickled vegetable category; kimchi is predominantly water, dietary fibre, and lactic acid with negligible digestible starch
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database (analogous estimate)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement for kimchi exists in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI ~15 is estimated by analogy with other lacto-fermented low-carbohydrate vegetables (pickled cabbage, sauerkraut) documented in the University of Sydney GI Database and peer-reviewed literature. Kimchi contains ~6g carbohydrate/100g of which ~3g is dietary fibre; digestible carbohydrate is ~3g/100g. Lactic acid produced during fermentation further suppresses glycaemic response. GL per 50g serving = (15 × 3g digestible carbohydrate per 50g) / 100 ≈ 0.45, rounded to 0.5. Confidence: 0.9.

hypertension.key_factor: Very high sodium density: 810mg sodium per 100g; a single 50g banchan serving delivers approximately 405mg sodium (~20% of the 2,000mg daily target).
hypertension.guidance: Hypertensive patients should limit kimchi to no more than one small portion (30g, ~240mg sodium) per meal; low-sodium kimchi variants should be requested where available, and kimchi must not be treated as a 'free vegetable' in sodium budgeting.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Very low glycaemic index (~15) and negligible glycaemic load (~0.5 per 50g serving); high dietary fibre (3g/100g) and lactic acid from fermentation further attenuate postprandial glucose response.
type2_diabetes.guidance: Kimchi is well-tolerated for people with Type 2 Diabetes at standard banchan portions (50g); its negligible glycaemic impact and probiotic content may modestly improve insulin sensitivity, though the high sodium content requires monitoring in patients with concurrent hypertension.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High sodium content (810mg/100g) is the primary cardiovascular concern; fat content is negligible (0.5g/100g, saturated fat 0.1g/100g) and cholesterol is low (5mg/100g).
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: For cardiovascular disease patients, kimchi's very low fat and cholesterol profile is favourable, but the high sodium density requires strict portion control (maximum 30–50g per meal) to avoid exceeding daily sodium targets and exacerbating hypertension-related cardiovascular risk.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Live Lactobacillus plantarum (~10⁸–10⁹ CFU per 50g serving) with potential anti-inflammatory effects via gut microbiome modulation; high sodium remains a limiting concern.
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Kimchi may offer modest anti-inflammatory benefit for rheumatoid arthritis patients through probiotic activity, but sodium intake must be monitored carefully, particularly in patients on corticosteroids or NSAIDs that affect fluid retention.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Kimchi (배추김치, baechu kimchi) is Korea's national fermented food and a UNESCO-registered Intangible Cultural Heritage (2013). It is made by salt-brining napa cabbage (baechu), then packing it with a spiced paste of gochugaru (Korean red pepper flakes), garlic, ginger, and salted shrimp or fish sauce, followed by lacto-fermentation at ambient or cool temperatures. The result is a tangy, spicy, crunchy condiment with a complex umami depth. Texture ranges from crisp (freshly made) to soft and intensely sour (aged). Kimchi is served as a banchan (side dish) at every Korean meal. Nutritionally, a 50g serving provides approximately 20 kcal, 0.75g protein, 3g carbohydrate, 1.5g dietary fibre, and 405mg sodium. The glycaemic index is approximately 15 (low), with a glycaemic load of ~0.5 per serving — negligible glycaemic impact. Key clinical consideration: sodium density is very high at 810mg/100g; hypertensive and cardiovascular patients must count kimchi carefully in their sodium budget. Not vegan or vegetarian (contains fish sauce and/or salted shrimp). Gluten-free and dairy-free. Live Lactobacillus plantarum and L. brevis (~10⁷–10⁹ CFU/g) provide probiotic benefit. Source: USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy FDC 169228, cross-checked with CJ Bibigo Singapore commercial label.

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced directly from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy FDC 169228 (Kimchi, raw), an approved primary source, and cross-validated against the CJ Bibigo Original Kimchi Singapore commercial product label. Core macros, secondary nutrients, and micronutrients are fully populated. GI is an analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database fermented vegetable category with documented rationale. Ontology codes (FoodOn, FoodEx2) are defensible and directly matched.
notes: 1. monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid breakdown for kimchi not reported in USDA SR Legacy FDC 169228 or CJ Bibigo Singapore product label. 2. water_g set to -1: moisture content not reported in the primary source consulted (USDA SR Legacy FDC 169228 nutrient panel as accessed). 3. trans_fat_g is 0.0 — confirmed zero per USDA SR Legacy FDC 169228 and CJ Bibigo product label. 4. vitamin_b12_mcg is 0.0 and vitamin_d_mcg is 0.0 — confirmed negligible/zero for plant-based fermented vegetable; trace B12 from fermentation microorganisms is below detection threshold in USDA data. 5. GI value of 15 is an analogous estimate; no direct GI measurement for kimchi exists in the University of Sydney GI Database as of the access date. 6. Halal status: commercial kimchi (CJ Bibigo, Daesang Jongga) sold in Singapore may not carry MUI/MUIS halal certification due to fish sauce and salted shrimp ingredients; is_halal set to false pending verified certification. 7. Kimchi is not vegan or vegetarian due to fish sauce and/or salted shrimp (saeujeot) in standard commercial formulations.
